annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: the Pythagorean theorem seems to be the most ancient and widespread mathematical development

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is formalized in inner-product-space angle form.

annotations.label: YBC 7289 approximation of √2

anchor.snippet: the Babylonian tablet YBC 7289 gives an approximation of

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes irrationality of √2 but not this numerical approximation.

annotations.label: Earliest statement of the Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: contain the earliest known statement of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: Same Pythagorean theorem statement is formalized.

annotations.label: Volume of a frustum

anchor.snippet: a method for finding the volume of a frustum

annotations.note: No frustum volume formula is formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Perfect number 6

anchor.snippet: perfect number theory (namely, that of the number 6)

annotations.note: Perfect numbers are defined as `Nat.Perfect`, and 6 satisfies that decidable predicate.

annotations.label: Solving first order linear equations

anchor.snippet: how to solve first order linear equations

annotations.note: Solving ax+b=0 follows trivially from field arithmetic but has no dedicated formalization.

annotations.label: Second-order algebraic equation (Berlin Papyrus)

anchor.snippet: ancient Egyptians could solve a second-order algebraic equation

annotations.note: The general quadratic formula `quadratic_eq_zero_iff` covers solving a quadratic equation.

annotations.label: Thales' theorem

anchor.snippet: deriving four corollaries to Thales' Theorem

annotations.note: Thales' theorem (angle in a semicircle is right) is formalized with an explicit alias.

annotations.label: First proof of the Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: the first proof of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem itself is proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Existence of irrational numbers

anchor.snippet: the proof of the existence of irrational numbers

annotations.note: Irrationality of √2 (`irrational_sqrt_two`) establishes existence of irrational numbers.

annotations.label: Method of exhaustion

anchor.snippet: Eudoxus developed the method of exhaustion

annotations.note: The method of exhaustion is a historical precursor to integration and has no direct formalization.

annotations.label: Axiomatic method (Euclid's Elements)

anchor.snippet: the format still used in mathematics today, that of definition, axiom, theorem, and proof

annotations.note: The axiomatic method is a methodology, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Irrationality of the square root of two

anchor.snippet: the square root of two is irrational

annotations.note: Directly formalized as `irrational_sqrt_two`.

annotations.label: Infinitude of primes

anchor.snippet: there are infinitely many prime numbers

annotations.note: Euclid's theorem is `Nat.exists_infinite_primes`.

annotations.label: Area under a parabola via infinite series

anchor.snippet: to calculate the area under the arc of a parabola with the summation of an infinite series

annotations.note: Infinite geometric series and integration are formalized, but Archimedes' parabola quadrature is not a specific result.

annotations.label: Archimedes' bounds for π

anchor.snippet: obtained the most accurate value of π then known

annotations.note: Mathlib has rigorous arbitrary-precision π bounds but not Archimedes' specific 96-gon values.

annotations.label: Surface area and volume of a sphere

anchor.snippet: his finding of the surface area and volume of a sphere

annotations.note: Volume of n-dimensional balls is formalized; sphere surface area is not a named result.

annotations.label: Conic sections from a cone

anchor.snippet: one can obtain all three varieties of conic section by varying the angle of the plane

annotations.note: Conic sections as cone-plane intersections are not formalized.

annotations.label: Sieve of Eratosthenes

anchor.snippet: devised the Sieve of Eratosthenes for finding prime numbers

annotations.note: No Sieve of Eratosthenes formalization was found.

annotations.label: Heron's formula

anchor.snippet: Heron's formula for finding the area of a scalene triangle

annotations.note: Heron's formula is not present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Menelaus' theorem

anchor.snippet: pioneered spherical trigonometry through Menelaus' theorem

annotations.note: Menelaus' theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ptolemy's theorem

anchor.snippet: Ptolemy's theorem for deriving trigonometric quantities

annotations.note: Ptolemy's theorem for cospherical points is formalized.

annotations.label: Diophantine equations

anchor.snippet: The study of Diophantine equations and Diophantine approximations

annotations.note: Many specific Diophantine equations (Pell, sums of squares, FLT cases) and Diophantine approximation are formalized, but not a unified theory.

annotations.label: Pappus's hexagon theorem

anchor.snippet: his hexagon theorem

annotations.note: Pappus's hexagon theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Pappus's centroid theorem

anchor.snippet: centroid theorem

annotations.note: Pappus's centroid theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Definitions of circumference, diameter, radius, volume

anchor.snippet: defined the concepts of circumference , diameter , radius , and volume

annotations.note: Diameter (`Metric.diam`), radius, and volume (Lebesgue measure) have Mathlib analogs but are not packaged as these classical definitions.

annotations.label: Proof of the Pythagorean theorem (Nine Chapters)

anchor.snippet: It created mathematical proof for the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem is proven in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gaussian elimination

anchor.snippet: a mathematical formula for Gaussian elimination

annotations.note: Gaussian elimination exists as an internal algorithm in the linarith tactic, not as a general linear-systems theorem.

annotations.label: Zu Chongzhi's approximation of π

anchor.snippet: Zu Chongzhi computed the value of π to seven decimal places

annotations.note: Arbitrary-precision π bounds are formalized, but no named seven-decimal value.

annotations.label: Cavalieri's principle (volume of a sphere)

anchor.snippet: a method which would later be called Cavalieri's principle to find the volume of a sphere

annotations.note: Volume of a ball is formalized, but Cavalieri's principle itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Horner's method

anchor.snippet: using a method similar to Horner's method

annotations.note: Horner-form recursion exists; the numerical evaluation algorithm is not a theorem.

annotations.label: Pascal's triangle (binomial coefficients)

anchor.snippet: a diagram of Pascal's triangle with coefficients of binomial expansions through the eighth power

annotations.note: Pascal's rule for binomial coefficients is `Nat.choose_succ_succ`.

annotations.label: Magic square

anchor.snippet: the complex combinatorial diagram known as the magic square

annotations.note: Magic squares are not formalized.

annotations.label: Squaring the circle approximation (Sulba Sutras)

anchor.snippet: constructing a circle with approximately the same area as a given square

annotations.note: This geometric approximate construction is not formalized.

annotations.label: Statement of the Pythagorean theorem (Sulba Sutras)

anchor.snippet: give a statement of the Pythagorean theorem

annotations.note: The Pythagorean theorem statement is formalized.

annotations.label: Binomial theorem (Pingala)

anchor.snippet: corresponds to an elementary version of the binomial theorem

annotations.note: The binomial theorem is `add_pow`.

annotations.label: Fibonacci numbers

anchor.snippet: the basic ideas of Fibonacci numbers

annotations.note: Fibonacci numbers are defined as `Nat.fib`.

annotations.label: Half-chord (sine) trigonometry

anchor.snippet: the first instance of trigonometric relations based on the half-chord

annotations.note: The sine function (the modern half-chord) is `Real.sin`.

annotations.label: Decimal place-value system

anchor.snippet: the decimal place-value system first appears

annotations.note: Positional base-b representation is formalized via `Nat.digits`.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta theorem

anchor.snippet: the Brahmagupta theorem

annotations.note: Brahmagupta's theorem on cyclic-quadrilateral diagonals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's identity

anchor.snippet: Brahmagupta's identity

annotations.note: Multiplicativity of the norm form x²−d·y² (`Zsqrtd.norm_mul`) is Brahmagupta's identity.

annotations.label: Brahmagupta's formula

anchor.snippet: Brahmagupta's formula

annotations.note: The cyclic-quadrilateral area formula is not formalized.

annotations.label: Zero as placeholder and digit

anchor.snippet: the use of zero as both a placeholder and decimal digit

annotations.note: Zero appears as a valid digit in the positional representation `Nat.digits`.

annotations.label: Mean value theorem (special case)

anchor.snippet: a special case of the mean value theorem in inverse interpolation of sine

annotations.note: Lagrange's mean value theorem is formalized; the interpolation case is an instance.

annotations.label: Pell equations (chakravāla method)

anchor.snippet: indeterminate equations of the type later known as Pell equations

annotations.note: Existence of nontrivial solutions to Pell's equation is formalized; the chakravāla algorithm itself is not.

annotations.label: Madhava–Leibniz series

anchor.snippet: found the Madhava–Leibniz series

annotations.note: The Leibniz series for π/4 is `tendsto_sum_pi_div_four`.

annotations.label: Madhava–Gregory series (arctangent)

anchor.snippet: the Madhava-Gregory series to determine the arctangent

annotations.note: The arctangent power series is `Real.hasSum_arctan`.

annotations.label: Madhava–Newton power series for sine and cosine

anchor.snippet: the Madhava-Newton power series to determine sine and cosine

annotations.note: Sine and cosine as power series are formalized (`Real.hasSum_cos`/`Real.hasSum_sin`).

annotations.label: Taylor series for sine and cosine

anchor.snippet: the Taylor approximation for sine and cosine functions

annotations.note: The Maclaurin/Taylor series of cosine and sine are formalized as `cos_eq_tsum`/`sin_eq_tsum`.

annotations.label: Algebraic solution of quadratic equations

anchor.snippet: the algebraic solution of quadratic equations with positive roots

annotations.note: The quadratic formula is `quadratic_eq_zero_iff`.

annotations.label: Al-jabr (reduction and balancing)

anchor.snippet: the transposition of subtracted terms to the other side of an equation

annotations.note: Equation balancing is intrinsic ring manipulation, not a named formalization.

annotations.label: Three nonlinear simultaneous equations

anchor.snippet: to solve three non-linear simultaneous equations with three unknown variables

annotations.note: This particular system of equations is not formalized.

annotations.label: Proof by mathematical induction (Al-Karaji)

anchor.snippet: Something close to a proof by mathematical induction appears in a book

annotations.note: Induction is a primitive recursor of Lean's type theory used throughout Mathlib, not a derived Mathlib theorem.

annotations.label: Sum of integral cubes

anchor.snippet: the sum of integral cubes

annotations.note: The identity ∑k³ = (∑k)² has no named Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Sum of fourth powers

anchor.snippet: to derive the formula for the sum of the fourth powers

annotations.note: A closed-form sum of fourth powers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Volume of a paraboloid by integration

anchor.snippet: an integration in order to find the volume of a paraboloid

annotations.note: Integration infrastructure exists but this specific volume is not a formalized result.

annotations.label: Geometric solution of cubic equations

anchor.snippet: the first to find the general geometric solution to cubic equations

annotations.note: Cubic polynomials and their discriminant are formalized; a geometric/radical solution is not.

annotations.label: Al-Kashi's value of π

anchor.snippet: computed the value of π to the 16th decimal place

annotations.note: Arbitrary-precision π bounds are available but no named 16-decimal value.

annotations.label: Algorithm for nth roots

anchor.snippet: an algorithm for calculating n th roots

annotations.note: nth roots exist as functions (rpow, Nat.sqrt) but the digit-extraction algorithm is not formalized.

annotations.label: Vigesimal (base-20) system

anchor.snippet: Maya numerals used a base of twenty, the vigesimal system

annotations.note: Base-b digit representation (b=20 as a special case) is formalized via `Nat.digits`.

annotations.label: Maya symbol for zero

anchor.snippet: the Maya developed a standard symbol for it

annotations.note: A historical numeral symbol is not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Bradwardine's rule of motion

anchor.snippet: speed (V) increases in arithmetic proportion as the ratio of force (F) to resistance (R) increases in geometric proportion

annotations.note: Bradwardine's kinematic rule is not formalized.

annotations.label: Instantaneous speed (Heytesbury)

anchor.snippet: proposed to measure instantaneous speed

annotations.note: Instantaneous rate of change is the derivative `deriv`.

annotations.label: Mean speed theorem

anchor.snippet: the distance covered by a body undergoing uniformly accelerated motion

annotations.note: The Merton mean-speed theorem of kinematics is not formalized.

annotations.label: Sum of odd numbers equals square numbers

anchor.snippet: the sum of the odd numbers are the square numbers

annotations.note: The identity ∑(2k+1)=n² has no named Mathlib lemma.

annotations.label: Solution of cubic equations

anchor.snippet: discovered solutions for cubic equations

annotations.note: Cubic polynomials and discriminants are formalized; Cardano's radical formula is not.

annotations.label: Solution of quartic equations

anchor.snippet: a solution for the quartic equations

annotations.note: No quartic solution formula is formalized.

annotations.label: Imaginary quantities (Bombelli)

anchor.snippet: how to deal with the imaginary quantities that could appear in Cardano's formula

annotations.note: Complex numbers are formalized as `Complex`.

annotations.label: Decimal notation (Stevin)

anchor.snippet: the first systematic treatment of decimal notation in Europe

annotations.note: Decimal-fraction notation as a system is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kepler's laws of planetary motion

anchor.snippet: Kepler succeeded in formulating mathematical laws of planetary motion

annotations.note: Kepler's laws of planetary motion are not formalized.

annotations.label: Analytic geometry

anchor.snippet: The analytic geometry developed by René Descartes

annotations.note: Coordinate/affine geometry (`EuclideanSpace`, affine spaces) embodies analytic geometry but is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Calculus

anchor.snippet: brought together the concepts now known as calculus

annotations.note: Differentiation (`deriv`) and integration (`MeasureTheory.integral`) are extensively formalized but 'calculus' is not a single decl.

annotations.label: Binary number system

anchor.snippet: He also refined the binary number system

annotations.note: Binary representation/recursion is formalized via `Nat.binaryRec` (and `Nat.digits 2`).

annotations.label: Probability theory and combinatorics

anchor.snippet: the investigations of probability theory and the corresponding rules of combinatorics

annotations.note: Both probability theory and combinatorics are formalized but span many declarations.

annotations.label: Pascal's wager

anchor.snippet: Pascal, with his wager

annotations.note: Pascal's wager is a decision-theoretic argument, not formalized.

annotations.label: Seven Bridges of Königsberg

anchor.snippet: founding the study of graph theory with the Seven Bridges of Königsberg problem

annotations.note: Eulerian trails and the even-degree condition are formalized; the Königsberg puzzle itself is not stated.

annotations.label: Imaginary unit i

anchor.snippet: he named the square root of minus 1 with the symbol i

annotations.note: The imaginary unit is `Complex.I`.

annotations.label: Fundamental theorem of algebra

anchor.snippet: the first satisfactory proofs of the fundamental theorem of algebra

annotations.note: Every nonconstant complex polynomial has a root (`Complex.exists_root`, `Complex.isAlgClosed`).

annotations.label: Quadratic reciprocity

anchor.snippet: quadratic reciprocity law

annotations.note: The law of quadratic reciprocity is formalized for the Legendre symbol.

annotations.label: Hyperbolic geometry

anchor.snippet: independently defined and studied hyperbolic geometry

annotations.note: The upper half-plane with its hyperbolic metric is formalized, but not a general hyperbolic-geometry framework.

annotations.label: Elliptic geometry

anchor.snippet: Elliptic geometry was developed later in the 19th century

annotations.note: Elliptic (spherical) geometry as such is not formalized.

annotations.label: Riemannian geometry

anchor.snippet: Riemann also developed Riemannian geometry

annotations.note: Riemannian distance via path length is formalized; full Riemannian geometry/curvature is not.

annotations.label: Manifold

anchor.snippet: he defined the concept of a manifold

annotations.note: Manifolds are formalized via `ChartedSpace`/`IsManifold`.

annotations.label: Vector spaces

anchor.snippet: gave a first version of vector spaces

annotations.note: Vector spaces are formalized as modules (`Module`) over a field.

annotations.label: Noncommutative algebra

anchor.snippet: developed noncommutative algebra

annotations.note: Hamilton's quaternions, the prototypical noncommutative algebra, are formalized.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: what is now called Boolean algebra

annotations.note: Boolean algebras are formalized as the `BooleanAlgebra` class.

annotations.label: Abel–Ruffini theorem

anchor.snippet: there is no general algebraic method for solving polynomial equations of degree greater than four

annotations.note: Mathlib's Abel-Ruffini theorem (radical-solvable ⟹ solvable Galois group), with `Equiv.Perm.not_solvable` supplying unsolvable degree-≥5 groups.

annotations.label: Impossible compass-and-straightedge constructions

anchor.snippet: straight edge and compass alone are not sufficient to trisect an arbitrary angle

annotations.note: Impossibility of angle trisection / constructible-number theory is not formalized.

annotations.label: Set theory

anchor.snippet: Georg Cantor established the first foundations of set theory

annotations.note: ZFC set theory is formalized via `ZFSet` (with `Cardinal` for cardinalities).

annotations.label: p-adic numbers

anchor.snippet: Kurt Hensel introduced p-adic numbers

annotations.note: The p-adic numbers are formalized as `Padic`.

annotations.label: Hilbert's 23 problems

anchor.snippet: set out a list of 23 unsolved problems in mathematics

annotations.note: Hilbert's list of problems is not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Four color theorem

anchor.snippet: proved the four color theorem

annotations.note: The four color theorem is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fermat's Last Theorem

anchor.snippet: proved Fermat's Last Theorem in 1995

annotations.note: The statement is defined and cases n=3,4 are proven (`fermatLastTheoremThree`/`fermatLastTheoremFour`), but the general theorem (Wiles) is not.

annotations.label: Independence of the continuum hypothesis

anchor.snippet: the continuum hypothesis is independent of

annotations.note: Independence of the continuum hypothesis is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Kepler conjecture

anchor.snippet: Thomas Callister Hales proved the Kepler conjecture

annotations.note: The Kepler sphere-packing conjecture is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Classification of finite simple groups

anchor.snippet: the classification of finite simple groups

annotations.note: The classification of finite simple groups is not formalized.

annotations.label: Kolmogorov's axiomatisation of probability

anchor.snippet: Kolmogorov 's axiomatisation of probability theory

annotations.note: Measure-theoretic probability (`MeasureSpace`/`IsProbabilityMeasure`) is Kolmogorov's axiomatization.

annotations.label: Lebesgue integral

anchor.snippet: Applications of measures include the Lebesgue integral

annotations.note: The Lebesgue integral is formalized as `lintegral` (and Bochner `integral`).

annotations.label: Hyperreal numbers

anchor.snippet: extending the field of real numbers to the Hyperreal numbers

annotations.note: The hyperreals are formalized as `Hyperreal`.

annotations.label: Surreal numbers

anchor.snippet: the surreal numbers were discovered by John Horton Conway

annotations.note: Surreal numbers / combinatorial game theory are not present in current Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lucas–Lehmer primality test

anchor.snippet: the Lucas–Lehmer primality test

annotations.note: The Lucas-Lehmer test is formalized (`lucas_lehmer_sufficiency`).

annotations.label: Decidability of Presburger arithmetic

anchor.snippet: the natural numbers plus either addition or multiplication (but not both), was decidable

annotations.note: Presburger-definable sets are characterized as semilinear (yielding decidability), and `mul_not_definable` captures the addition-but-not-multiplication boundary.

annotations.label: Gödel's incompleteness of Peano arithmetic

anchor.snippet: known as Peano arithmetic , was in fact incomplete

annotations.note: Gödel numbering exists in Mathlib but the incompleteness theorems are not formalized.

annotations.label: Millennium Prize Problems

anchor.snippet: announced the seven Millennium Prize Problems

annotations.note: The list of Millennium Prize Problems is not a formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Poincaré conjecture

anchor.snippet: the Poincaré conjecture was solved by Grigori Perelman

annotations.note: The Poincaré conjecture is not formalized.

annotations.label: Green–Tao theorem

anchor.snippet: the Green–Tao theorem

annotations.note: The Green-Tao theorem on primes in arithmetic progression is not formalized.

annotations.label: Bounded gaps between primes

anchor.snippet: existence of bounded gaps between arbitrarily large primes

annotations.note: Zhang/Maynard bounded-gaps results are not formalized.

annotations.label: Modularity theorem

anchor.snippet: the modularity theorem

annotations.note: The modularity theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: AKS primality test

anchor.snippet: The AKS primality test was published in 2002

annotations.note: The AKS primality test is not formalized.

annotations.label: Goldbach's weak conjecture

anchor.snippet: A proof of Goldbach's weak conjecture was published by Harald Helfgott

annotations.note: Goldbach's weak conjecture is not formalized (the only 'Goldbach' in Mathlib concerns Fermat-number coprimality).

annotations.label: Fundamental lemma (Langlands program)

anchor.snippet: proofs of the fundamental lemma

annotations.note: The Langlands fundamental lemma is not formalized.
